wins: Separation of FedEx Freight to unlock significant value for stockholders
Strong Q2 results with sequential improvement in DRIVE savings and adjusted operating profit
Successful implementation of DRIVE program with $540 million in savings
Positive outlook for the second half of FY '25 with adjusted EPS range of $19 to $20
Enhanced customer experience through improved service and technology
risks: Soft industrial economy negatively affecting B2B volumes
Challenging demand environment impacting freight segment
Uncertainty around the demand environment affecting future performance
Potential customer attrition due to business separation
